signal: Runtime correcto localizado en orquestador_de_agentes; validacion limpia y memoria lista para continuar el trabajo.

WP-2026-137: Placing lock acquisition inside a method (bootstrap) that is called from multiple call sites (run_reactive, run_loop, standalone) created a double-call regression. Second call hit FileExistsError with own PID alive -> returned False -> caller exited immediately without running. Fix: reentrancy flag (_supervisor_lock_held) so same instance re-enters transparently. Rule: when a lock-acquiring method is reused across call sites, guard with an instance-level reentrancy flag before the OS-level O_CREAT|O_EXCL check.

WP-2026-137: Changing a method return type from None->bool (bootstrap) requires updating callers from "if not method():" to "if method() is False:". The falsy guard caused a test regression: existing test monkeypatched bootstrap to "lambda: None", so "if not None" was True and run_reactive exited immediately. Rule: when a method previously returned None and is refactored to return bool, always use "is False" guards in callers to avoid false-positive exits on None-returning mocks or legacy callers.

BLOCKER pattern: Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard. When a method acquires an exclusive resource (O_CREAT|O_EXCL, flock, Lock.acquire, lock-file creation) AND can be reached from more than one call site or called twice on the same instance (e.g. standalone call + internal call from a wrapper), there must be an explicit instance-level reentrancy guard. Without it, the second call hits the exclusion check with its own PID alive and returns False, silently aborting the caller. Manager should grep all call sites of the method in the diff and repo. No reentrancy guard = CHANGES. Origin: WP-2026-137 bug audit.

BLOCKER pattern: Boolean truthiness regression in changed return contracts. When a method changes from returning implicit None to returning explicit bool, all callers must be updated from generic truthiness guards (if not x, if x, while x) to identity checks (is False, is True). Mixing None/False/True under a falsy guard silently breaks when the method is monkeypatched to return None (common in tests) or called from a legacy path that predates the type change. Manager must grep all callers in the diff and verify no if not / if pattern remains. Any surviving generic guard = CHANGES. Origin: WP-2026-137 bug audit.

deliverable_type classification for scaffolding tickets: when Files Likely Touched contains only structural non-Python files (.gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic, the correct deliverable_type is documentation, not code. Using code triggers ruff+pytest rubric which produces false noise on files with no logic. Manager should flag code classification for pure-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS). Origin: WP-2026-133 audit.

Validator evidence gate: when a work_plan explicitly declares a validator as a quality gate (skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), the Manager must find explicit output from that validator showing a clean result in execution_log.md. Declared validator + absent evidence = BLOCKER. This applies especially to scaffolding and documentation tickets where standard code gates do not run automatically. Origin: WP-2026-133 audit.

Structural complexity predicts regression risk better than file count. Tickets that apply the same atomic operation N times (e.g. create .gitkeep in 7 dirs) carry near-zero regression risk regardless of file count — majority-scaffolding scope = light review. Tickets that change behavior across multiple call sites or layers carry high risk even with few files — cross-layer behavior changes = deep review. Use as context signal when calibrating review depth, not as a hard blocker criterion. Origin: WP-2026-133 vs WP-2026-137 contrast.

The smithery.ai python-code-review skill uses a diff-based approach: the Manager reviews ONLY changed files (via git diff) instead of the full workspace. This reduces noise and focuses the review on actual risk surface. Potentially worth a dedicated WP to restrict Manager review context to Files Likely Touched + their direct callers. Current approach reviews whole plan context which can produce observations about unchanged code. Extracted from external skill audit on 2026-05-25.

The smithery.ai python-code-review skill organizes project memory into 4 explicit categories: project_overview, common_patterns, known_issues, review_history. Our current observations.jsonl is flat. Adding a category/topic taxonomy could make retrieval and MAX_RUBRIC_OBSERVATIONS selection more precise (e.g. pull only known_issues for code reviews). Worth evaluating as an evolution of observations.jsonl schema. Extracted from external skill audit on 2026-05-25.

AP-08 candidate: Test coverage drift. The Builder runs the existing suite, sees it pass, and declares quality gates satisfied — but the new functions introduced in the diff have no direct tests. The suite passing is not evidence of coverage when the new code is never called by any test. Manager rule: when the diff introduces new functions (def, method, classmethod), verify that at least one test in test_*.py calls each new function directly. Absence of direct test coverage for new functions = BLOCKER, even if the full suite passes. Origin: WP-2026-139 audit — 3 new methods (_parse_canonical_anti_patterns, _load_canonical_anti_patterns, _render_canonical_anti_pattern_inventory) had zero tests; Manager approved without noticing.

Pattern: index-file + inline-instructions separation. When a system needs both a human-readable canonical inventory (IDs + short names, in a markdown file) and LLM-optimized detection instructions (detailed English text with examples, in code), keep them as separate artifacts with explicit roles. The file is the single source of truth for IDs and names; the code references those IDs in its detailed rubric. Extracting the path to a dedicated method (_canonical_anti_patterns_path) makes it trivially monkeypatcheable in tests. Fallback: OSError -> warnings.warn(RuntimeWarning) + return [] keeps the system online with degraded but functional output. Origin: WP-2026-139 review_bridge canonical AP loading.

Skills schema v2.0.0 established: 4 required frontmatter fields added to all 22 SKILL.md files — role (builder|manager|shared|user), stage (setup|plan|implement|review|quality|close|memory|meta|support), writes_memory (bool), quality_gate (bool). validate_all.py enforces enums and bool types. Dirs starting with _ excluded from discovery (convention: _shared/ = non-skill shared infrastructure). Origin: session 2026-05-25.

AP-NN numbering system established as cross-cutting convention linking 3 artifacts: skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md (canonical source of IDs + names), bui-implement-from-plan/references/code-rules.md (Builder preventive rules, detailed examples), man-review-implementation/references/review-checklist.md (Manager BLOCKER checklist). Adding a new AP requires touching all 3. observations.jsonl can carry anti_pattern_id field to link findings to AP-NN. AP-01..AP-08 active. Origin: session 2026-05-25.

Convention: every BLOCKER in Manager review-checklist.md has a corresponding preventive rule in Builder code-rules.md at the same AP-NN ID. The mirror is intentional: Manager detects, Builder prevents. When a new AP is added, both surfaces must be updated in the same commit. A BLOCKER without a preventive rule in code-rules.md is an incomplete AP. Origin: session 2026-05-25.

Auto-improvement loop architecture documented and operational: (1) human audit finding -> (2) observations.jsonl entry with applies_to scope -> (3) review_bridge._render_manager_review_learnings() injects into Manager prompt -> (4) Manager detects pattern in future tickets -> (5) session-close-observations promotes recurring signal to AP-NN -> (6) AP-NN appears in code-rules.md (Builder) and review-checklist.md (Manager). The loop is closed. review_bridge also loads AP IDs from _shared/anti-patterns.md with lazy cache at __init__. Origin: session 2026-05-25.

Policy: ruff extend-exclude should point to tests/sandbox/ (not tests/) so the test suite is linted. Use per-file-ignores in pyproject.toml to suppress systematically legitimate patterns (S603, S607, S108, SIM115, PERF203) as policy rather than per-line noqa. Rationale: per-file-ignores survive ruff autoformat; per-line noqa on try: lines get stripped. Origin: WP-2026-140.

Bus import boundary firewall implemented: tests/test_bus_boundary.py enforces bus/ -> scripts/ seam via (1) AST static analysis (ast.walk ImportFrom/Import) and (2) grep-based dynamic import detection (importlib.import_module, __import__). Only allowed seam: scripts.discover_skills. ALLOWED_SCRIPTS_IMPORTS set is the extension point for future approved seams. Origin: WP-2026-140.

Pattern: on Windows, files edited by Python tools (Edit/Write) may have mixed CRLF+LF endings. pre-commit mixed-line-ending hook fixes them but fails if there are unstaged files (stash+restore conflict). Fix: run python -c to convert all .py files to LF before git add, so the hook finds nothing to change. Origin: WP-2026-140 commit session.
WP-2026-145 validated that a bus-first STATE projection probe can detect real drift: bus=READY_TO_CLOSE while STATE.md remained IN_PROGRESS before canonical closeout.

Minor debt discovered in the projection probe: _parse_markdown_state accepts ticket_id but does not use it yet; this is safe today because STATE.md is single-ticket, but it would become a silent bug if the file ever became multi-ticket.

WP-2026-146: Builder reused manager_review.timeout_seconds (180s, AI subprocess latency) as the HUMAN_GATE expiry timeout. These serve different actors on completely different time scales: model latency (seconds) vs human availability (hours). Fix: separate config key human_gate_timeout_seconds with 86400s fallback. Rule: when two timeouts share a config section but serve actors with different time scales, they must have distinct keys — never reuse a latency timeout for a human-availability timeout.

WP-2026-146: Builder generated unique IDs with sha256(ticket_id + timestamp), requiring time.sleep(0.01) in tests to avoid collisions. Rule: use uuid4() for IDs that must be unique within a process — it is collision-free by definition, removes sleep dependencies from tests, and does not leak timing information.

WP-2026-147: graphify indexed uv-cache, _archive, reviews — 97% noise nodes — because IGNORE_DIRS lacked system-state dirs. Rule: after adding any file scanner, bucket nodes by path prefix and verify signal/noise ratio before using the output.

WP-2026-147: ticket originally mixed a read-only adapter (graph_context.py) with a full pipeline build (project_scanner.py). Corrected before Builder started. Rule: read-only adapter over existing artifacts and new infrastructure pipeline are different risk profiles — split into separate tickets even when they share a theme.

WP-2026-149: placing a new side-effecting sync call at the top of main() before flag dispatch would run it on every invocation, not only the named handlers. Rule: when the plan names specific handlers (for example main status path and --validate), the implementation must call the sync inside those handlers and nowhere earlier in the dispatcher.

WP-2026-149: the plan explicitly required editing an existing function (_materialize_state_transition) but the diff initially only added new code. Rule: if a plan names a legacy function or regex to be modified, the resulting diff must include that exact legacy edit; new tests alone are not sufficient evidence of compliance.

WP-2026-152: state_from_review_decision maps changes->IN_PROGRESS, making the --request-changes guard reject it unconditionally. Fix: derive pending_requeue from events[-1] before the guard. Pattern: any guard on bus_state must account for state machine transitions that map review decisions to in-flight states.

subprocess.run with capture_output=True silently discards stderr/stdout unless returncode is checked. Pattern: always store subprocess result and log stderr to sys.stderr on rc != 0, especially for state-transition subprocesses where silent failure breaks re-engagement chains.

AP-09: WP-2026-154: guard_paths.py __main__ read tool_calls/shell_command (assumed keys) instead of tool_input/command (real Claude Code PreToolUse protocol). The same wrong assumption propagated into the integration tests, which also used tool_calls format — so production and tests reinforced each other's error. The hook always exited 0, functionally identical to the old stub. Rule: before reading any key from an external payload, locate the protocol spec and verify the exact key name. If a hook unconditionally exits 0 under all inputs, it is probably reading the wrong key.

AP-10: WP-2026-154: TestGuardHookProfiles created synthetic hook scripts in tmp_path that loosely mimicked guard_paths.py behavior, then ran subprocess against those surrogates. The real guard_paths.py was never invoked by any integration test. All 3 integration tests passed while the real hook had critical input-format and fail-open bugs. Rule: integration tests must invoke the real module/script — find its path and use it directly. If the test never imports or calls the real artifact, it tests the surrogate, not the code.

AP-11: WP-2026-154: guard_paths.py resolved unknown strictness_profile with profiles.get(profile_name, profiles.get('standard', {})) — silent fallback to standard when the named profile was absent. A partially migrated or hand-edited agents.json could silently reduce enforcement to base-only level. Rule: security gates must fail closed (exit 2 / raise) on invalid or unknown config. Silent permissive fallback in a security gate is more dangerous than an explicit block.

WP-2026-157: the safe suite did not include tests/test_guard_paths.py and tests/test_configuration_loading.py, so an import regression in test compatibility was only caught during manual review. Rule: when a regression can occur in a small, stable test file, include that file in the safe suite or explicitly document why it is excluded.

AP-12: WP-2026-157: the review packet built from git diff HEAD hid brand-new untracked files, so the Manager saw an incomplete/partial diff while the real deliverables lived outside the tracked set. Rule: review packets must include new untracked deliverables explicitly, not only tracked-file diffs.

WP-2026-157: exposing evaluate_tool_request() and requeue_ticket() for eval coverage required refactors in guard_paths.py and bus/supervisor.py that were outside the original Files Likely Touched list. Rule: when the plan requires in-process evals, declare the production refactor surface explicitly so scope gating does not reject necessary testability changes.

WP-2026-159: when the supervisor process stays alive while code has already changed on disk, it can keep running the pre-fix requeue logic and miss the new CHANGES trigger path until the process is restarted. Rule: after modifying supervisor code that affects requeue or liveness, restart the supervisor process before validating the next CHANGES cycle.

WP-2026-159: the Builder prompt that told a fresh window to 'emit BUILDER_EXIT' manually caused a hallucinated close command (--emit-exit builder ...) instead of the intended mark-ready flow. Rule: closeout instructions in new Builder windows must point only to python .agent/agent_controller.py --mark-ready --json --force and never invent a manual BUILDER_EXIT command.

WP-2026-160 exposed a contradictory sequence in the ticket design: the plan had to define a cooperative exit path for the old supervisor and a deterministic wait path for the launcher. Rule: a ticket must never ask two phases to impose opposite actions on the same resource without a single explicit handoff mechanism.

WP-2026-160 showed that a criterion like 'observable and fail closed' is not enough by itself. Rule: every acceptance criterion must name a concrete verifier (test, command, stderr string, or file assertion) so the Manager can approve without inference.

WP-2026-160 only became reviewable when PLAN and AUDIT matched the same sequence, signals and observables. Rule: if the audit asks for a signal that the plan does not name exactly, the ticket is still ambiguous and must be revised before approval.

WP-2026-161 review: the TP Check section was placed after the 7-artifact handoff checklist in SKILL.md, making it appear optional. An agent reading linearly would complete the handoff before seeing the gate. Rule: any approval gate must appear as an explicit numbered step before the execution flow, not as a standalone section at the end. Fixed by adding step 0 to the handoff checklist.

pre-commit end-of-file-fixer muto .agent/context/project-map.json durante un flujo de pre-push, causando un push fallido hasta excluir el artefacto generado. Rule: los artefactos generados bajo .agent/context/ deben quedar fuera de hooks de whitespace y formateo que reescriben archivos.

ruff format reformateo archivos Python en pre-push, abortando el push y obligando a separar los hooks mutadores de los verificadores. Rule: formateadores mutadores solo en pre-commit; pre-push debe ser verificacion pura.

HANDOFF_BLOCKED es una senal de bloqueo intencional del Builder antes de --mark-ready, no un crash del proceso. El supervisor ya tiene RELAUNCH_SUPPRESSED para suprimir el relaunch en este caso. Tratar HANDOFF_BLOCKED como crash y relanzar el Builder es incorrecto: el Builder esta vivo y bloqueado, no muerto.
expected_next_event: RELAUNCH_SUPPRESSED tras HANDOFF_BLOCKED; si no aparece, hay bug en supervisor
recommended_followup: Verificar que bus/supervisor.py suprime relaunch cuando el ultimo evento del ticket es HANDOFF_BLOCKED; anadir test de integracion que valide la supresion

signal: Cuando el supervisor emite BUILDER_RELAUNCH_ATTEMPTED y el launcher reporta exito, puede ocurrir que la ventana del Builder se abra pero el proceso opencode interior falle silenciosamente. El supervisor no detecta este caso porque solo verifica el exit code del .ps1, no la vida del proceso interior.
trigger_sequence: BUILDER_RELAUNCH_ATTEMPTED (seq=86/88) sin BUILDER_EXIT posterior en plazo razonable
expected_next_event: BUILDER_STARTED (no existe aun) o BUILDER_EXIT en menos de 5 min; su ausencia es el sintoma del fallo silencioso
recommended_followup: Abrir WP futuro: Builder emite BUILDER_STARTED al arrancar; supervisor espera ese evento con timeout tras cada BUILDER_RELAUNCH_ATTEMPTED y reintenta o alerta si no llega

signal: El comando --session-close no actualiza PROJECT.md ni CHANGELOG.md. Estos archivos siempre requieren una fase manual en tickets de cierre de sesion. Cada vez que se abre un WP de cierre, se redacta una Fase 2 que recrea esa logica. Si el skill session-close o project-finalize incluyera un checklist explicito, el WP se reduciria a ejecutar un skill en lugar de redactar fases manuales repetidas.
trigger_sequence: WP-175 Fase 2 redactada manualmente para cubrir lo que --session-close no hace
expected_next_event: Skill session-close o project-finalize con checklist explicito de PROJECT.md y CHANGELOG.md
recommended_followup: Enriquecer skills/project-finalize/SKILL.md con: (1) que escribe el Builder en PROJECT.md (version, ultimo ticket, estado), (2) que entrada añade en CHANGELOG.md (fecha, tickets del ciclo, estado), (3) comando de archivo de artefactos. Elimina la necesidad de Fase 2 manual en futuros WP de cierre.

signal: Ticket WP-2026-175 completado: ** Canonical session closeout and cycle rollover (deliverable_type=unknown)
Decisiones arquitectonicas documentadas en WP-2026-175

Cierre canonical: STATE.md, execution_log.md and TURN.md must reflect the same closeout contract. IDLE works as a workspace sentinel with no active ticket, but it is not a valid motor validator state; use COMPLETED or the terminal enum accepted by the controller when closing a ticket.
impact: prevents false closes and validator mismatch
recommended_followup: Add to closeout_lessons.md. Check that STATE.md, execution_log.md, work_plan.md and TURN.md all agree before --manager-approve.

signal: IDLE is a workspace-level sentinel, not a validator-facing terminal state. When closing tickets, do not write IDLE into motor-facing state files; reserve it for surfaces that mean no active ticket.
impact: avoids invalid closeout state and keeps validator green
recommended_followup: Add to closeout_lessons.md. IDLE is a workspace sentinel only; use COMPLETED or APPROVED for motor ticket states.

signal: review_queue.md is a traceability surface, not a manual scratchpad. Preserve it as history for the full review cycle and let the code manage it; do not prune or hand-edit entries during closeout.
impact: preserves review provenance and avoids manual drift
recommended_followup: Add to closeout_lessons.md. review_queue.md is a live surface written by the bridge; do not edit manually.

signal: --bootstrap-ticket emits STATE_CHANGED -> IN_PROGRESS (not APPROVED). APPROVED is the documental state in STATE.md and work_plan.md, set by the human when approving a plan. IN_PROGRESS is the bus state for an active Builder. A plan that says 'use --bootstrap-ticket to emit STATE_CHANGED -> APPROVED' is wrong: the flag will emit IN_PROGRESS, and a Builder checking the bus for APPROVED evidence will fail to find it. Rule: when planning a Fase 0 bus alignment, use IN_PROGRESS as the target state, not APPROVED.

Bus-first read authority: for operational launch/relaunch decisions, derive ticket state from the event bus first and treat TURN.md/STATE.md as fallback or documentary projections only. This hardening closed the TURN drift failure class addressed in WT-2026-216. Any new launch, resume or recovery path must consult the bus before markdown projections.
recommended_followup: Apply when designing any new operational helper that needs to know which agent to launch.

signal: Canonical-consumer recovery: when a critical bus trigger outlives its main consumer, the durable fix is to ensure the canonical consumer runs again rather than adding a parallel authority. WT-2026-212 applied this: review_bridge forces a real supervisor tick (bootstrap + run_once) after REVIEW_DECISION=CHANGES instead of relaunching Builder directly.
recommended_followup: Apply when designing recovery paths for any bus trigger that could lose its consumer.

signal: Runtime cleanup and bus reconciliation are different classes of recovery. If the previous ticket is already terminal in the bus, clean stale local runtime only; if the previous ticket is non-terminal with confirmed drift, reconcile explicitly in the bus with reconcile_ticket.py; if bus state is unreadable or contradictory, abort without closing anything. Mixing these classes risks emitting spurious terminal events to the bus.
recommended_followup: Encode these three cases explicitly in WT-2026-214 preflight spec before implementation.

signal: In agent-assisted development, an agent self-report is a hypothesis, not evidence. Accept only verifiable artifacts such as diffs, exit codes, test output, bus events, commits or byte-level checks.
A false green is critical debt: guards, hooks and tests must be validated by injecting the failure they claim to block, especially when they protect high-trust surfaces.

When tests and production disagree, classify before changing: compare the test, committed production behavior and canonical contract. Sometimes the right fix is production, sometimes the right fix is the test.
CEM rigor must be proportional to blast radius and reversibility. Derive the tier mainly from touched paths and system surface instead of the Builder self-assessment.
Deferred debt is acceptable only when explicit, counted and tied to an exit criterion. Strict allowlists that fail when entries become clean are the preferred pattern for cleanable debt.

A Builder relaunch preserves autonomy only if root/topology are verified first and the new window receives a fresh evidence-linked handoff capsule derived from canonical sources. Context continuity is the antidote to relaunch amnesia.

La raiz del repo_motor debe contener solo producto portable y reusable: codigo, prompts, tests, scripts, docs de producto y configuracion del motor. El historial operativo del dogfooding (PLAN_*, AUDIT_*, checkpoints de review, artefactos de ticket) pertenece al repo_destino bajo .agent/collaboration/ o .agent/collaboration/archive/. No commitear artefactos operativos del destino en el root del motor.

En repo_motor, un ID de ticket en el nombre de un archivo versionado es senal de historia operativa o naming legacy, pero no autoriza borrado automatico. Clasificar antes de actuar: producto reusable -> renombrar a nombre funcional; historia operativa unica -> archivar en repo_destino y retirar del motor; temporal ignorado -> eliminar solo tras human gate; ambiguo -> bloquear y pedir decision humana. Aplica a filenames versionados, no a menciones dentro de codigo, changelog, docs o commits. Enforced por _check_versioned_filenames en scripts/session_closeout.py.

Solo json_final_answer es fuente autoritativa para APPROVE o CHANGES en review_bridge. json_last_text, json_no_decision y text_regex son diagnosticos: si producen una decision fuerte, degradar a INSPECT. Riesgo concreto: text_regex escanea stdout completo, incluida la plantilla del review packet con literales DECISION: APPROVE/CHANGES que son instrucciones, no veredicto real. Protege contra falso APPROVE y requeue ciego por CHANGES vacio.

Un validador que solo registra errores sin cambiar el resultado del flujo es fail-open. Patron observado en _validate_changes_structure(): el validator detectaba CHANGES invalido, pero el caller no degradaba la decision. Regla para agentes: el caller de un validator de decision debe mutar estado/decision o bloquear explicitamente cuando el validator falla.

Launcher PowerShell under Set-StrictMode must not access ConvertFrom-Json/PSCustomObject properties via direct dotted chains ($config.permission, $obj.child.prop) or assume collection aggregates like .PSObject.Properties.Name. Use $obj.PSObject.Properties['name'] with null checks, create missing properties explicitly, iterate collections explicitly, and add a functional test under Set-StrictMode -Version Latest with a minimal real JSON fixture — parse-only and textual tests will not catch this.

For code or mixed tickets in repo_motor/repo_destino topology, preflight must verify five packaging invariants before Builder launch: Files Likely Touched covers every expected code/test/config artifact; transient .opencode/opencode.json modifications injected by the launcher are restored before --pre-handoff runs, not after the Builder window closes; quality gates point to real existing paths; commit evidence references the exact active ticket ID, including suffixes like WT-2026-236a; and every file visible in git status --short for repo_motor belongs to the current ticket's Files Likely Touched. Lateral changes from prior sessions such as memory uploads, prompt edits, smoke artifacts, or unfinished Builder work will land in the review packet and trigger scope drift even when the implementation itself is correct. These changes must be committed separately, stashed, or cleaned before assembling the code ticket review packet.

In repo_motor plus repo_destino topology, any fallback or stub that silently degrades behavior when motor_root is absent, such as fail-open stubs for agent spec resolution, path discovery, or config injection, violates the topology contract. These stubs may unblock tactical progress or pass isolated tests, but Manager review for a code ticket that formalizes the topology will correctly treat them as architectural blockers. Rule: in topology-aware code, either fail closed with an explicit error when motor_root is absent, or extract the compatibility fallback to a dedicated ticket with explicit scope and human approval. Shipping a tactical stub as part of a topology-hardening code ticket is fragility disguised as delivery.

Every plan starts from a completed `...a` ticket. Tickets `...b`, `...c`, `...d` and later letters are reserved for planned splits or for fixes discovered after the `...a` closeout. There is no valid plan without a closed `...a` anchor.

When a shell-launched Builder leaves the bus short of canonical termination, the durable path is root-cause analysis first, then chat closeout of the `...a`, then remediation through derived tickets. Do not repair the bus through the live bus; use controlled commits and chat for bus-state recovery to avoid self-contamination.

In repo_motor plus repo_destino topology, the tracked .opencode/opencode.json must remain path-agnostic and portable. Workspace-specific external_directory permissions for the active repo_destino are runtime state, not product configuration: the launcher may inject them temporarily for the Builder session, but it must restore the original file before pre-handoff/review and they must never be committed as absolute local paths. If a ticket needs those permissions, treat it as OpenCode permission preflight plus runtime injection, or add them locally in repo_destino/.opencode/ if that destination owns local overrides.
